explicit_text: I recall the early days of skiing near Sky Tavern, where the first rope tow was just a motor at the bottom and a big wheel halfway up the hill. We skied with old hickory skis and primitive equipment compared to today. Sky Tavern marks the location where we first experienced this new way of skiing, and it was a significant place for our early ski adventures.

explicit_text: After graduating from officer's school in January 1943, I volunteered for the 10th Mountain Division ski troops and trained at Camp Hale, Colorado. We were specialized in mountain and winter warfare, carrying heavy equipment and skiing in tough conditions. The division saw combat in Italy, breaking through the Gothic Line and advancing through the Po Valley before returning to the U.S. for planned amphibious operations that were canceled after the atomic bombs were dropped.

explicit_text: We went into combat just outside the walled city of Lucca, Italy, in January 1945. It was there that the 10th Mountain Division began breaking through the German Gothic Line. Lucca was the starting point of our rapid advance through northern Italy.

explicit_text: We were stalled for almost a week on the west side of Lake Garda because the Germans had a battery of 88mm cannons positioned there. While there, we heard Mussolini was on the other side of the lake in his villa. I took a patrol across the lake in an amphibious truck and found the villa just hours after Mussolini had left.

explicit_text: While stationed near Lake Garda in 1945, we learned Mussolini was staying in a villa on the other side of the lake. I led a patrol there and found the villa only four hours after he and his girlfriend had left. Shortly after, partisans captured him and strung him up. I even brought back some of his gold filigree cap emblems as souvenirs.

explicit_text: We were aboard the USS Marine Fox near the Azores Islands when we heard the news that the first atomic bomb had been dropped on Hiroshima. It was unbelievable to think such destruction was possible. A few days later, the second bomb was dropped on Nagasaki, and our planned invasion of Japan was called off.

explicit_text: After the war, I struggled to find work until Marsh Johnson at Johnson Chevrolet offered me a job on the condition I study accounting under the GI Bill. I took a correspondence course for three years, got straight As, and was proud of my achievement. Johnson Chevrolet was the place where I rebuilt my civilian career.

explicit_text: After returning from the war and struggling with civilian life, I found myself at the Riverside Hotel in Reno, quietly drinking alone. It was a low point, but it also sparked a turning point where I talked to myself and decided to get organized and find a job. The Riverside Hotel was where I realized I needed to change my life.
